subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Install the newest versions of all packages currently installed on the system

subcommands.name: dist-upgrade
subcommands.description: Same as upgrade, but also handles changing dependencies with new versions of packages

subcommands.name: dselect-upgrade
subcommands.description: Follows the changes made by dselect(1) to the Status field of available packages

subcommands.name: purge
subcommands.description: Same as remove, but also deletes configuration files

options.name: --compile
options.description: The package will be compiled to a binary .deb

options.name: --download-only
options.description: The source package will not be unpacked

subcommands.name: build-dep
subcommands.description: Install/remove packages in an attempt to satisfy the build dependencies for a source package

subcommands.name: check
subcommands.description: Updates the package cache and checks for broken dependencies

subcommands.name: download
subcommands.description: Download the given binary package into the current directory

subcommands.name: clean
subcommands.description: Clears out the local repository of retrieved package files

subcommands.name: autoclean
subcommands.description: Same as clean, but only removes package files that can no longer be downloaded

subcommands.name: autoremove
subcommands.description: Remove packages that were automatically installed to satisfy dependencies for other packages and are no longer needed

subcommands.name: changelog
subcommands.description: Displays the changelog of a package

subcommands.name: indextargets
subcommands.description: Displays a listing of information about all data files (aka index targets) apt-get update would download

options.name: --no-install-recommends
options.description: Do not consider recommended packages as a dependency for installing

options.name: --install-suggests
options.description: Consider suggested packages as a dependency for installing

name: -d
--download-only
options.description: Package files are only retrieved, not unpacked or installed

name: -f
--fix-broken
options.description: Attempt to correct a system with broken dependencies in place

name: -q
--quiet
options.description: Produces output suitable for logging, omitting progress indicators

name: -s
--simulate
--just-print
--dry-run
--recon
--no-act
options.description: Perform a simulation of events that would occur based on the current system state but do not actually change the system

name: -V
--verbose-versions
options.description: Show full versions for upgraded and installed packages

name: -a
--host-architecture
options.description: Controls the architecture packages are built for by apt-get source --compile and how cross-builddependencies are satisfied
